representativeInputs.visualSemanticNote: This is source identity and sequence context for a monthly Emini setup slide pack, useful for routing the ZIP image package into the chart-pattern encyclopedia rather than for a standalone lesson rule.
This is a section marker for failed bullish expectations and trapped-participant concepts, not a full teaching example by itself.
Routes to double-top flag review, climactic second-leg behavior, breakout-test context, and trend continuation versus reversal literacy.
Routes to low-two flag recognition, range-bottom context, EMA interaction, and failed continuation or retest literacy.
Routes to exhaustion after a late bull climax, transition into sideways behavior, and the idea that a strong late move may not immediately reverse.

completionRule: The module knowledge map is complete when all 386 AI-absorbed Course 5 follow-up cards are assigned into module-level evidence rows with representative inputs and next actions.
boundary: Private education-operations module map for Course 5 AI absorption. It organizes internal knowledge evidence by module, not learner-facing lessons, source-folder removal approval, production readiness, live execution cues, return promises, execution-platform integration, automation, or real-world trading guidance.
